description: ThousandEyes capabilities for banks, investment firms, insurance companies, payment processors, and fintech companies

common_requirements.answer: Partial. ThousandEyes maintains SOC 2 Type II certification and security controls; PCI DSS compliance requires additional security assessment and configuration.
Yes. ThousandEyes maintains SOC 2 Type II certification demonstrating comprehensive security and operational controls.
Partial. ThousandEyes provides audit logging and data retention features supporting FINRA requirements; specific compliance validation needed.

common_requirements.requirement: Data encryption at rest and in transit
common_requirements.answer: Yes. ThousandEyes encrypts all data in transit via TLS 1.2+ and at rest using AES-256 encryption.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors trading platforms with sub-second granularity, measuring latency, jitter, and packet loss for market connectivity.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors market data feeds through network and application testing for availability and performance.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors exchange connectivity with network path analysis and latency measurement from trading venues.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors online banking applications including login flows, transaction processing, and account access with synthetic testing.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors mobile banking backend APIs and services supporting iOS and Android applications.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors ATM network connectivity and transaction processing systems through network and application testing.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors payment gateway APIs for availability, response time, and transaction completion rates.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors card processing networks and payment processors through API testing and transaction validation.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors ACH and wire transfer systems through application testing and network connectivity validation.

common_requirements.requirement: Core banking system performance (Temenos, Fiserv, FIS)
common_requirements.answer: Yes. ThousandEyes monitors core banking platforms through HTTP/API testing and database connectivity validation.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors real-time payment systems including RTP and Zelle for availability and transaction speed.
Yes. ThousandEyes provides end-to-end monitoring for branch connectivity including WAN, VPN, and application access.
Yes. ThousandEyes Endpoint Agents monitor VPN performance including connection quality, latency, and application access for remote workers.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors credit bureau API connectivity and response times through synthetic testing.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors fraud detection APIs and services for availability and response time performance.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors Salesforce Financial Services Cloud for availability, performance, and user experience.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors Microsoft 365 services including Teams, Outlook, and SharePoint for financial services teams.
Yes. ThousandEyes monitors failover scenarios across data centers with automated testing and alerting on failover events.
Partial. ThousandEyes monitors database connectivity and response time but does not monitor internal replication lag; integrates with database monitoring tools.
Yes. ThousandEyes provides real-time alerting with immediate notification upon threshold breach via email, SMS, webhook, and integrations.
Yes. ThousandEyes provides customizable executive dashboards with SLA reporting and performance metrics for leadership visibility.
Yes. ThousandEyes integrates natively with ServiceNow for automated incident creation, event correlation, and CMDB integration.
Yes. ThousandEyes provides native Splunk integration for security monitoring, log correlation, and compliance reporting.
Yes. ThousandEyes supports hybrid deployment with Enterprise Agents in private cloud and on-premise data centers.
Yes. Cisco provides 24/7 premium support with tiered SLAs based on severity for ThousandEyes customers.
